query: How often has Trump made tariff threats against China in 2025-2026? What is the frequency of his China tariff threats - how many weeks or months has he gone without threatening China tariffs?
answer: The structured output has been provided with the complete chronological timeline, gap analysis, and key frequency findings for Trump's China tariff threats from January 2025 through May 2026.

key_facts: Trump made at least 18-21 distinct China-specific tariff threats, escalations, or major trade actions from January 2025 through May 2026.
The longest gap without a voluntary China tariff threat was approximately 14 weeks (July 3 to October 10, 2025), during the post-Geneva truce period.

The longest total gap between any China-relevant tariff events was approximately 16 weeks (November 1, 2025 to February 20, 2026), but this was broken by a Supreme Court ruling rather than a voluntary Trump threat.
During the most intense escalation phase (April 2-12, 2025), there were 6 separate China tariff escalations in just 10 days.
Post-deal pauses typically lasted 5-11 weeks before a new threat or provocation emerged.
Trump has never gone a full 4 months (17+ weeks) without either threatening, imposing, or restructuring tariffs affecting China.
The pattern is cyclical: escalation → deal → quiet period (5-14 weeks) → new threat/provocation → escalation.

Peak tariff rate on China reached 245% on some goods (April 17, 2025); the Geneva truce cut this to 30% (May 14, 2025); the Busan deal cut to ~20-25% (October 30, 2025); SCOTUS ruling reset to ~10% baseline (February 20, 2026).
During active escalation periods (Feb-Apr 2025), China tariff actions occurred every 3-7 days on average.
As of May 21, 2026, the US and China are in a conciliatory phase following the Trump-Xi Beijing Summit (May 14-15, 2026), with tariff aggression redirected toward the EU.
The most recent China-specific tariff threat was April 8, 2026 (50% tariff threat over alleged Iran arms shipments), approximately 6.3 weeks ago.
100% tariff on Chinese EVs (Biden-era Section 301) remained in effect throughout the entire period regardless of other tariff changes.
